title: [lichen_util.h:62] secure_zero strict aliasing violation
description: Critic: Line 62 casts 'volatile uint8_t *p' to 'volatile unsigned long *' and dereferences it. This violates C strict aliasing rules (uint8_t and unsigned long are incompatible types). While functionally correct when the buffer was originally an unsigned long array, it is UB when zeroing a struct or char array. Fix: use memcpy to store the zero value, or use 'char *' which is allowed to alias any type, or compile with -fno-strict-aliasing.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced strict-aliasing-violating cast with __builtin_memcpy + volatile read-back
